content.problemStatement:
Consider a list LLL of permutations, where a permutation with length xxx denotes a permutation of integers 111 through xxx and the elements of this list are indexed starting from 111. Initially, the list contains a single permutation with length 111 (i.e. L1=(1)L_1 = (1)L1​=(1)).

You may create new permutations and append them to LLL using operations with the following form:


Choose two (not necessarily distinct) valid indices iii and jjj.
Let's denote the permutations LiL_iLi​ and LjL_jLj​ by (A1,A2,…,AS)(A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_S)(A1​,A2​,…,AS​) and (B1,B2,…,BR)(B_1, B_2, \ldots, B_R)(B1​,B2​,…,BR​) respectively.
Create a new permutation in one of the following ways (note that the permutations LiL_iLi​ and LjL_jLj​ in the list LLL are not actually changed):

Add SSS to each element of LjL_jLj​, then concatenate LiL_iLi​ and LjL_jLj​, i.e. the resulting permutation is C=(A1,A2,…,AS,B1+S,B2+S,…,BR+S)C = (A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_S, B_1 + S, B_2 + S, \ldots, B_R + S)C=(A1​,A2​,…,AS​,B1​+S,B2​+S,…,BR​+S). For example, if Li=(1,4,2,3)L_i = (1, 4, 2, 3)Li​=(1,4,2,3) and Lj=(2,1)L_j = (2, 1)Lj​=(2,1), then C=(1,4,2,3,6,5)C = (1, 4, 2, 3, 6, 5)C=(1,4,2,3,6,5).
Add RRR to each element of LiL_iLi​, then concatenate LiL_iLi​ and LjL_jLj​, i.e. the resulting permutation is C=(A1+R,A2+R,…,AS+R,B1,B2,…,BR)C = (A_1 + R, A_2 + R, \ldots, A_S + R, B_1, B_2, \ldots, B_R)C=(A1​+R,A2​+R,…,AS​+R,B1​,B2​,…,BR​). For example, if Li=(1,4,2,3)L_i = (1, 4, 2, 3)Li​=(1,4,2,3) and Lj=(2,1)L_j = (2, 1)Lj​=(2,1), then C=(3,6,4,5,2,1)C = (3, 6, 4, 5, 2, 1)C=(3,6,4,5,2,1).


Append CCC to LLL, i.e. if LLL contained ppp permutations before this operation, then CCC becomes permutation Lp+1L_{p+1}Lp+1​.
You are given a permutation P=(P1,P2,…,PN)P = (P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_N)P=(P1​,P2​,…,PN​). You should create this permutation using at most 2N2N2N operations (the last of these operations must append PPP to LLL) or determine that it is impossible. It is not required to use the minimum number of operations.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, if it is impossible to create the given permutation, print a single line containing the string `"NO"` (without quotes). Otherwise:


Print a line containing the string `"YES"` (without quotes).
Then, print a line containing a single integer KKK — the number of operations you want to perform (1≤K≤2N1 \le K \le 2N1≤K≤2N).
Then, print KKK lines. Each of these lines should contain three space-separated integers iii, jjj and www describing one operation (1≤w≤21 \le w \le 21≤w≤2), where w=1w=1w=1 or w=2w=2w=2 denotes that you want to create the new permutation in the first way or the second way (described above), respectively.
Your solution will be considered incorrect if either iii or jjj is invalid for any of your operations or if at the end, the last permutation in the list is not equal to PPP.
